title: atocion armeria
intro: Atocion armeria, also known as the thorny toadflax, is a plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its clusters of pink or purple flowers and sharp, thorny stems. The medicinal properties of Atocion armeria are primarily derived from its roots, which contain iridoid glycosides, compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and indigestion, as well as respiratory problems, including coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Atocion armeria can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to promote digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
